meaning: prep.虽然,尽管adv.尽管,还是 conj.虽然,尽管
examples: The bad weather notwithstanding, the event was a great success.
He despised William Pitt, notwithstanding the similar views they both held.
Notwithstanding some major financial problems, the school has had a successful year.
The bearish headlines have gotten bolder, and the panic has gotten worse (notwithstanding the late-November rebound).
If they agree, they can sue your employer on your behalf notwithstanding your arbitration agreement.
